petitpals.title: PetitPals Web & Extension
petitpals.subtitle: A full-stack universal baby registry ecosystem designed for the modern fragmented e-commerce landscape.

stats.reach_desc: Universal compatibility with 100% of online storefronts.
stats.onboarding_desc: Time from discovery to first meaningful user action.
stats.conversion_desc: Successful reservation rate on shared public registries.

sections.overview_content: PetitPals addresses the 'fragmented cart' problem where parents' desired items are scattered across niche boutiques, Etsy, and major retailers. The challenge was building a high-trust 'source of truth' that unifies these data points into a cohesive, aesthetically premium registry. By removing the friction of multi-site management, we transition the user from a chaotic spreadsheet to a radical, unified gifting experience.
sections.architecture_content: The system follows a decoupled architecture, bridging a Chrome Extension (Manifest V3) with a React 19 administrative dashboard via a shared Supabase backend. I adopted a Feature-Sliced Design (FSD) to encapsulate complex reservation logic away from parent-facing management tools. This modular approach allows for rapid scaling of the scraper engine without risking the stability of the core financial and reservation state.
sections.engineering_content: A primary engineering hurdle was maintaining state synchronization between separate browser contexts without introducing high-latency polling. I architected a reactive bridge using Postgres Change Data Capture (CDC) to ensure that saves made in the extension reflect in the dashboard in under 100ms. I prioritized memory management by implementing strict AbortController signals to prevent leaks during heavy DOM-scraping sessions in the extension.
sections.engineering_explanation: Leveraging Supabase Realtime to synchronize global state across the Browser Extension and Web App contexts with sub-100ms latency.
sections.ai_content: To scale the universal parser, I developed an AI-assisted pipeline that generates adaptive DOM-scraping templates for diverse e-commerce schemas. Instead of manually writing selectors for every site, LLMs identify structural patterns in unknown storefronts to normalize product metadata instantly. This strategy significantly reduced time-to-market for retailer compatibility while maintaining high data accuracy.
sections.tradeoffs_content: I implemented a custom Shadow DOM wrapper for the extension UI to ensure complete style isolation from host websites, sacrificing bundle size for visual consistency. While a standard iframe would have been easier, the Shadow DOM allowed for a more seamless 'native-to-site' feel that users associate with high-end tools. Additionally, I chose Client-Side Rendering (CSR) for the reservation flow to prioritize low-latency interactions over traditional SEO, as most registry traffic is direct and referral-based.
sections.impact_content: By identifying that guest friction was the #1 cause of lost gift reservations, I engineered a 'zero-login' checkout system using signed tokens for secure, one-click claims. This architectural pivot resulted in a 45% increase in guest conversion rates compared to legacy registry models. The technical focus on 'radical simplicity' directly translated into measurable user growth and platform stickiness.
